revision.text:
[[ja:阿波村 (茨城県)]][[ko:아바촌 (이바라키현)]] {{Former Japanese municipality | Abolition date = September 30, 1956 | Reason for abolition = Merged into [[Sakuragawa Village (Ibaraki)|Sakuragawa Village]] | Details of abolition = '''Awa Village''' → '''[[Sakuragawa Village (Ibaraki)|Sakuragawa Village]]''' | Current municipality = [[Inashiki]] | Kana = あばむら | Municipality name = Awa Village | Category = Village | Prefecture = Ibaraki Prefecture | District = [[:en:Inashiki District|Inashiki District]] | Area = | Population = | Population source = | Population date = 1956 | Neighboring municipalities = [[Edosaki]], [[Ōsuga Village (Ibaraki)|Ōsuga Village]], [[Higashi Town (Ibaraki)|Higashi Village]], Sakuragawa Village | A11ress = Awa, Inashiki District, Ibaraki | Coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:city_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Awa Village|qq=}} | Position image = | Notable points = }} '''Awa Village''' (あばむら) was a village in [[:en:Inashiki District|Inashiki District]], {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, Japan. == Geography == * Located in central [[Inashiki City]], it formed the southern part of the former [[Sakuragawa Village (Ibaraki)|Sakuragawa Village]]. * The village consisted of valleys with interspersed plateaus and plains. == History == === Transition of jurisdiction === * [[April 1, 1889]] – Under the [[Municipal System]], [[Awa (Inashiki)|Awa]], [[Jingūji (Inashiki)|Jingūji]], [[Amada (Inashiki)|Amada]], [[Shika (Inashiki)|Shika]], [[Minamiyamaku (Inashiki)|Minamiyamaku]], and [[Sukatsu (Inashiki)|Sukatsu]] merged to form '''Awa Village''' in [[Kawachi District (Ibaraki)|Kawachi District]]. * [[April 1, 1896]] – Kawachi District merged with [[Shinjo District]] to form '''[[:en:Inashiki District|Inashiki District]]''', and Awa Village became part of Inashiki District. * [[September 30, 1956]] – Awa Village ceased to exist due to municipal mergers: ** Parts of Awa Village were incorporated into [[Higashi Village (Ibaraki)|Higashi Village]]. ** The remaining area of Awa Village was merged into [[Sakuragawa Village (Ibaraki)|Sakuragawa Village]]. '''Transition Table''' {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:small" ! colspan="2" | Pre-1868 ! April 1, 1889 ! colspan="2" | April 1, 1896 ! September 30, 1956 ! 1973 ! 1978 ! September 1, 1996 ! March 22, 2005 ! Current |- | rowspan="9" | [[Kawachi District (Ibaraki)|Kawachi District]] | bgcolor="#99CCFF" | Awa Village | bgcolor="#99CCFF" rowspan="9" | '''Awa Village''' | rowspan="9" | [[:en:Inashiki District|Inashiki District]] | bgcolor="#99CCFF" rowspan="9" | '''Awa Village''' | rowspan="8" | Sakuragawa Village | rowspan="7" | Sakuragawa Village | rowspan="6" | Sakuragawa Village | rowspan="6" | [[Sakuragawa Village (Ibaraki)|Sakuragawa Village]] | rowspan="9" | Inashiki City | rowspan="9" | [[Inashiki City]] |- | bgcolor="#99CCFF" | Jingūji Village |- | bgcolor="#99CCFF" | Amada Village |- | bgcolor="#99CCFF" | Shika Village |- | bgcolor="#99CCFF" | Minamiyamaku Village |- | bgcolor="#99CCFF" | Sukatsu Village |- | bgcolor="#99CCFF" | Parts of Jingūji | Higashi Village
incorporated | rowspan="3" | Became a town |- | bgcolor="#99CCFF" | Parts of Amada | Higashi Village
incorporated | rowspan="2" | [[Higashi Village (Ibaraki)|Higashi Village]] |- | bgcolor="#99CCFF" | Parts of Awa | Higashi Village
incorporated | Higashi Town |} == Population and Households == === Population === '''Total [Unit: Persons]'''
{{ill|1891|en|1891|qid=Q7837|lt=1891|1=1891}}[[File:g100.png]][[File:g100.png]][[File:g10.png]][[File:g10.png]][[File:g10.png]][[File:g05.png]][[File:g01.png]][[File:g01.png]][[File:g01.png]][[File:g01.png]] 2,394
{{ill|1920|en|1920|qid=Q2155|lt=1920|1=1920}}[[File:g100.png]][[File:g100.png]][[File:g50.png]][[File:g10.png]][[File:g10.png]][[File:g01.png]][[File:g01.png]] 2,721
{{ill|1935|en|1935|qid=Q18658|lt=1935|1=1935}}[[File:g100.png]][[File:g100.png]][[File:g50.png]][[File:g10.png]][[File:g10.png]][[File:g10.png]][[File:g05.png]][[File:g01.png]][[File:g01.png]][[File:g01.png]][[File:g01.png]] 2,893
{{ill|1950|en|1950|qid=Q18597|lt=1950|1=1950}}[[File:g100.png]][[File:g100.png]][[File:g100.png]][[File:g50.png]][[File:g05.png]][[File:g01.png]][[File:g01.png]] 3,574
=== Households === '''Total [Unit: Households]'''
1920[[File:r50.png]][[File:r05.png]] 555
1935[[File:r50.png]][[File:r01.png]][[File:r01.png]][[File:r01.png]][[File:r01.png]] 545
1950[[File:r50.png]][[File:r10.png]][[File:r01.png]] 617
== Transportation == === Roads === * '''[[Secondary National Highway|Secondary National Road]]''' ** [[:en:National Route 125|National Route 125]] == References == * Kadokawa Japanese Place Name Dictionary Editorial Committee, ''Kadokawa Japanese Place Name Dictionary Vol. 8: Ibaraki Prefecture,'' Kadokawa Shoten, 1983. ISBN 4040010809 == Related Articles == * [[List of abolished municipalities in Ibaraki Prefecture]] * [[Awa Village (disambiguation)]] {{Japan-area-stub|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|阿波村 (茨城県)|version=83433158|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:阿波村 (茨城県)]] [[ko:아바촌 (이바라키현)]] {{wikidata link|Q11657550}} [[Category:Japanese Cities and Towns Established in 1889]] [[Category:Japanese municipalities abolished in 1956]] [[Category:河内郡 (茨城県)]] [[Category:稲敷市域の廃止市町村]] [[Category:稲敷郡]]
